data.epi_content:
# Erica's Rampage

Even after escaping, I continued to rob villages here and there.

Honestly, I wondered how someone like me could rob villages so easily, but after thinking about it, I realized I was just looting empty houses since all the soldiers across the continent had gathered to capture Evan.

From a certain cliff, I could barely see the Capital where the Academy was located, but I observed countless soldiers swarming around it. Looking carefully, I noticed that everything surrounding the walls was human.

Despite being extremely far away, I could hear the roaring cheers and even louder screams, and I found myself overwhelmed by the sight.

Well, I had never seen so many humans clashing at once before.

"Ivan, this is quite a spectacle."

I dismounted from Ivan, put tobacco in my mouth, and lit it. In the past, it would have taken me a while to light that tiny flame, but now it ignites instantly thanks to the grateful sacrifices that died by my hand.

That one dot floating in the air, frantically throwing fireballs, must be Vivian. I can't see clearly, but it's probably her.

After all, there's probably no other human capable of using such powerful magic besides Vivian. Seeing this, it seems I've half-succeeded in escaping that hellish life.

"The genre has changed. From romance to ridiculous comedy, hehehe."

Still, it seems the protagonist remains Vivian.

"So my goal is to escape from her, settle somewhere, and live peacefully..."

I wonder if that's possible. It looks like about a hundred people burn with each fireball she throws. Though it might be fine since most of the soldiers are skeletons clattering around behind me.

"I said I'd try to save Evan this time, but I wonder if I made a mistake."

Even the time spent worrying feels wasteful. I stubbed out my tobacco on the ground, threw it away, and had the skeletons toss a spark onto the pile of fallen leaves I'd gathered. One leaf began to burn, and the fire spread instantly.

I've realized recently that arson is surprisingly efficient. Though fires often fail to catch, once they do, countless people living in the mountains, as well as animals and plants, burn to death.

"Let's go, Ivan. Anywhere with people."

I climbed back onto the dog with a struggle and began wandering aimlessly.

Evan would laugh if he saw this. After all, a noble lady riding a dog is utterly ridiculous.

...

"A witch! A witch has invaded the village...!"

This time, an old man who spotted me riding the dog from a distance began alerting others with shouts. I quickly shot him in the forehead, but judging by the screams and the movement of many people, I was slightly too late. Why was he just loitering outside the main gate taking a stroll?

Still, I should make use of whoever remains.

With that thought, I entered the village, where two handsome-looking men were waiting for me. One held a sword, the other a staff.

These people still haven't realized that modern magic wands aren't those rustic wooden things but metal weapons with gunpowder. They must be out of touch with current trends.

"So you're that black magician's whore..."

After creating the ridiculous scene of riding a dog through the village gate, the bespectacled magician addressed me in a slightly low voice.

"That old man who just died, you could have saved him. Why didn't you?"

I interrupted him and asked a question.

"What nonsense are you talking about after killing him yourself?"

"Did you think I would stop the bullet to save that old man, preventing you from entering? Or did you want to create a frightening scene so people in the village could escape faster? Well, that useless old man was just wasting food anyway."

Are these Vivian's potential boy toys? She probably sent them to stop me. When at a disadvantage, they don't answer and just glare, just like her. Slaves really do resemble their masters, don't they?
data.epi_content2:
I have so much to show Vivian when I meet her. I've learned some magic too, so I can show her what Evan showed me. Though I doubt Vivian would be swayed by seeing it.

Anyway, winning is difficult. So I scattered all the corpses I'd collected into the air and slapped Ivan's behind to start running away. Those bodies were three weeks old, and the stench was absolutely foul.

...

I recall the face of that old man who shouted that a witch had appeared when he saw me before I entered the village. That expression of someone detached from death, someone who knew they would die but had to do what needed to be done, bothered me.

Saving others through self-sacrifice is too noble. It feels disrespectful to even compare myself to that, so I covered my face with my hands and collapsed sideways. Ivan licked my face and then lay down beside me. Using Ivan as a pillow was comfortable.

...

"Please, at least spare my child! Please save my baby!!"

A woman clutching a baby in her arms begged me, rubbing her hands together. The woman was so emaciated that her facial bones were visible, making her look like a skeleton covered in human skin.

I stared at the bundle she was holding for a long time. Though there were no maggots, flies were circling around it, and the child was already dried up and dead.

"Alright. But you..."

The woman nodded, smiling through tears as if this was the natural course of action. I made it painless by instantly blowing her head apart.

...

"You murderer! Devil! Get out of here right now!!"

When I entered another village, it was completely empty except for two crippled men.

"You've been abandoned."

"No, I chose to stay. If I remain here, you might at least..."

I didn't want to hear it, so I crushed his head immediately.

...

In the next place I visited, a city too large to be called a village, executions were in full swing. Prisoners with black hoods over their heads were hanging in the air, frantically kicking their legs as if dancing. After finishing their dance, they seemed to lose all strength, which would be fine if not for the terrible smell.

A festival was taking place where people threw stones at someone placed in a pit, so I joined in by throwing stones at the heads of the participants. The humans who had been laughing while throwing stones at another person apparently didn't like being hit themselves and fled screaming. Though it was meaningless since skeletons surrounded the city.

I approached the girl who had been pelted with stones.

"Child, what's your name?"

Her mouth was split open and her head was dented, so she couldn't speak. I used healing magic on her. Then she grabbed her head and sobbed for a long time.

"It's because of you! I almost died because of you!"

I was taken aback by this sudden blame directed at me. When I asked for an explanation, she answered:

"I almost got stoned to death because I have the same name as you and silver hair!"

Feeling inexplicably upset, I killed all the humans in the city. Including the girl sitting in front of me.

Of course, I didn't do it directly. The white skeletons worked hard with their clubs. I think there will be many skeletons with shattered skulls this time.

...

One child.

Two men.

Four women.

Ten elderly.

...
data.epi_content3:
Eight men.

One woman.

The scent of night flowers.

...

Seven children.

Pigs, probably twenty humans.

Blood, fishy smell, night flower scent.

...

Three elderly.

One pot.

A child well-boiled in hot water.

...

A hundred sheep.

Two dogs.

One elderly person.

...

Twenty-seven men.

Two women.

Something that used to be a woman in a pot.

...

"UWEEEEEK!! UWEEK!! EGEEEK!!!"

All kinds of nausea overwhelm me, and tears pour from my eyes. My insides feel blocked, and I'm suffocatingly frustrated.

I was literally hunting, that's for certain. Damn it, there's nothing that could be called human.

"I'm supposed to be the black magician. The cursed devil, or whore. Why do they treat others even more cruelly than I do?"

Evan, how long did Evan go around doing these things? I'm reaching my limit.

By now, shouldn't Vivian have come to stop me?

No, I just want to stop now. I'm sick of killing, whether it's people, beasts, or livestock.

"Ivan, Ivan. There are no real humans in this world. Except for Evan and me, they were all just disgusting beasts wearing masks. No matter how hungry you are, eating children is going too far, isn't it?"

I would rather die. That's what I would have done.

I don't consider myself a person with exceptional morality or altruism. But there should be some minimum standard, right?

"Or is it that everyone became like this because of me?"

But if they all transform like that just from being hungry, it's better they don't exist in this world.

"No, it's because Vivian took all the normal humans away. That's why I'm seeing these disgusting sights while remaining in the Empire."

Right. I'm just looting empty houses. Only those kinds of people remain. Normal humans probably fled west or north long ago, leaving behind the disabled, the elderly, and burdensome children in the villages.

I'm just a cleaner, an eagle, a crow, a hyena that feeds on such things. Gathering strength by consuming what someone else has already discarded, things that are already mentally dead...

Suddenly my thoughts were interrupted.

"Erica, isn't it about time you stopped?"

Vivian appeared before me.
